title: Bike Touring Vlog - Serbia - Belgrade to Bosnia. At Sunny Resort. 5-26-2010

transcript.text: Okay, so this place I'm staying, in case I ever get famous, I can tell them I was here.
It's called the Sirkana Ruka.
The Sunny River Sports and Recreation Complex.
And they have all of those activities for the children.
Trail running, aww.
And horseback riding.
And all that good stuff.
And supposedly Robert De Niro named his daughter after this river right here.
Pretty.
Check this out, this is the third time somebody's given me free food for free.
This dude at a pizza place bought me a cake in Austria.
And then in Croatia, this girl staying at my hostel, this Greek girl, is like,
Oh, you bike tour!
We should hang out, I want to do bike tour!
But I was about to leave, so she doesn't buy me a piece of cake.
But check this out.
Serbians are cool.
Dear Kyle,
We are very glad that you are our guest.
We wish you good luck, good health, and nice weather on your exceptional bike tour through Europe.
Maybe it is your destiny to come at the place where your famous De Niro spent several nights long time ago when he was a teenager.
Take this bread and coamac as a sign of Serbian hospitality.
Good luck, Biba, and Zoran Mirkovic.
Look what they gave me.
They gave me dumplings.
I love these.
I love these.
I love these.
I love these.
These little sausages and french fries and salad with the flower-shaped tomato, a cake, and another piece of bread with utensils.
Wow, Serbians are cool, huh?
Groovy, groovy.
This is Spike Blog, May 27th, 2010.
I'm in Serbia, just on the Serbian side of the border with Serbia and Bosnia, right into Sarajevo tomorrow.
I'm probably like 100 miles from Sarajevo, here's my room, it's a pretty cool room, I have my own cabin.
Camping is 10 euros, there's my shower.
I have to wait 20 minutes for it to warm up.
Camping was 10 euros and this was 18 euros, so I figured I'd take this because I'm sick now, I think.
So check it out, there's my cabin, and I'm next to this river that looks like it's about to overflow, man.
Seriously, and it's going really fast here.
It seems like some kind of retreat area.
We've got horses for kids to go on, and a bunch of little kids, and a beach over there, and a bunch of playgrounds.
I was on the road, and I was like cracking, and I was dying, and I was like, I saw this place, and I was like, let me just stop here.
You know, I'm probably spending more money on this place than I would if I just went 15 more kilometers down the road,
And there's no internet, and the TV pretty much doesn't work, but that's fine because I feel like crap.
Today's a full moon, I was going to ride 24 hours, 30 hours, 35 hours today, but I'm sick, dude.
It's like a fever, and I feel like puking, um, I learned a valuable lesson today, and that's to stay the fuck away from McDonald's, man.
Yesterday I ate, like, three Big Mac meals or something, and then, and then, I think I had one the day before that,
And then this morning I had a Big Mac meal, and then I had another Big Mac, and then I started riding, and I left town,
And I felt like something was seriously wrong with me. I felt like puking, and I was even dry heaving a little bit,
And I haven't eaten a single thing all day, and I feel like if I put something in my mouth, I'll just puke it up.
I saw a video last night about, um, the trash heap in the Pacific, and they, you know, they trawled,
And even though it looks totally normal, you know, normal ocean, they trawled it,
And there were six times as much plastic particles in the water as, um, algae, plankton,
And, like, all the birds and fish are eating that stuff, and then they're dying because their bellies are getting filled up with plastic.
Like, that's kind of how I felt today. Like, I filled my belly up with plastic.
Like, the plastic cheese from the Whopper or whatever, or the Big Mac, and I was like, fuck that.
I have to have healthy food, and all I was thinking about was healthy food.
While I was writing down, I was like, oh man.
I guess when I first went into Belgrade, I got this really nice meal of, like, tomato soup.
It was such good tomato soup, and it's like, just to have that would just be amazing.
I'm thinking, after I have my shower, I'll be able to put something good in me.
But, I barely, I'm like sick.
I think I'll be okay tomorrow if I just, um, if I just sleep the rest of the day and have a nice big healthy meal.
And, um, there's another possible reason why I could be sick.
Like, I think I have a science experiment growing in my water bottles, because I've been drinking juice.
I think drinking juice is a good thing to do when you're bike touring, or bicycling in general,
If you don't have access to super drinks like Gatorade.
Because it's easy calories, but, uh, and it's a lot of calories, too.
Like, one little juice has the same pretty much amount of calories as a power bar, so.
But, um, there's this, like, layer of, like, black particles growing on the side,
And you can't, and you can't clean them off the water bottle.
Like, if you put water in there and shake it really hard, it doesn't come off at all.
The only way to get it off is to wipe it off.
And so, I think I'm going to start wiping my water bottles off every night.
Because there could be light critters growing in there that I'm drinking.
But I think the main reason I don't feel good is because I ate at McDonald's, man.
So I'm going to stay away from McDonald's.
Plus, I took three days off, and that might have been a bad idea.
I don't do well if I don't ride for a long time, and I had a lot of lactic acid, and I don't know.
Plus, I was drinking a lot of alcohol.
Anyways, I feel, I don't feel good.
But, so, no more, no 24-hour ride for me, but I think I'll go into Sarajevo.
I was going to skip Sarajevo, and Sarajevo is supposed to be a good place to tour.
They take this out.
It's the Robert De Niro Hut.
Probably because Robert De Niro stayed there.
They had the Jimi Hendrix Hut on the Annapurna Circuit in Nepal.
Because Jimi Hendrix stayed there.
So, okay.
See ya.
